cost.question: How much does a product engagement cost?
cost.answer: Product engagements are typically structured as fixed-price discovery (1–2 weeks) followed by phased fixed-price builds. Total project cost varies enormously by scope, but typical mid-sized products land between €60K and €250K from kickoff to launch. Long-term iteration is a monthly retainer.

timeline.question: How long until we launch?
timeline.answer: Most products reach a shippable v1 in 12–16 weeks. Smaller MVPs can launch in 8–10. Enterprise products with complex integrations and compliance can take 6 months. We don't promise dates we can't hit.

team.question: Who owns the product after launch?
team.answer: You do. We can stay on as a long-term operations partner if you want, or we can transfer cleanly to your internal team with documentation, knowledge transfer sessions, and 30 days of post-handoff support.

pricing.question: Do you do equity deals?
pricing.answer: Rarely. We've done equity-only or cash + equity blends with a small number of early-stage clients where we believed strongly in the team. Default is cash. We're an engineering firm, not a venture studio.

maintenance.question: Do you maintain products after launch?
maintenance.answer: Yes. Most clients keep us on a monthly retainer for ongoing iteration, performance work, security patches, and feature development. Some clients hand off to their internal team — both work.

ownership.question: Who owns the code, design, and IP?
ownership.answer: You do. Source code, design files, infrastructure-as-code, documentation, test suites — all transferred to you under your IP. We retain the right to use generic, anonymized techniques in future engagements.

hero.titleLine1: We tell you what we think.
hero.titleLine2: Whether you hired us for it or not.
hero.subtitle: Architecture review, AI strategy, build-vs-buy analysis, modernization roadmap. We are senior engineers who ship in production — not slide-deck consultants. The advice you get is the advice we'd take ourselves.

areas.title: Five areas where engineering judgment matters most.
areas.subtitle: We advise on the decisions that are hard to reverse — architecture, build-vs-buy, AI adoption, modernization. The ones where the wrong choice costs you years.

architecture.title: Architecture review
architecture.description: Independent review of an existing system or a planned design. We read the code, walk the data flow, identify the highest-risk decisions, and write a report you can act on without us.
architecture.outcome: A 30-50 page written report with prioritized findings.

buildvsbuy.title: Build vs buy analysis
buildvsbuy.description: Should you build that auth system, or use Auth0? Should you build that data pipeline, or use Fivetran? We do the math against your specific context — team size, growth projection, vendor lock-in cost.
buildvsbuy.outcome: A clear written recommendation with the math shown.

aistrategy.title: AI strategy & roadmap
aistrategy.description: Most companies are wasting AI budget on the wrong things. We help you identify the 2-3 places where AI actually moves the needle — and the 10 places where it doesn't.
aistrategy.outcome: An AI roadmap with prioritized use cases and ROI estimates.

modernization.title: Modernization roadmap
modernization.description: You have a legacy system that nobody wants to touch. We map the safest incremental path forward — strangler-fig migration, dependency extraction, team-by-team rollout.
modernization.outcome: A phased modernization plan with risk estimates per phase.

team.title: Engineering team topology
team.description: Your team is growing and the org chart is starting to show its weak points. We help you design team structures, ownership boundaries, and hiring sequences that scale.
team.outcome: A team topology document with hiring priorities.

process.title: Three steps. No slide decks.
process.subtitle: We don't show up with a generic framework. We read your code, talk to your team, and write a report that's specific to your situation.

read.title: Read your code
read.description: We get repository access (or NDA-protected snapshots), walk the codebase, run the tests, instrument the data flows. No assumptions. No 'best practices'. Just understanding what's actually there.

interview.title: Interview your team
interview.description: 30-minute structured conversations with the engineers who built it, the leaders who own it, and the operators who run it. We surface what people actually think but rarely get to say.

write.title: Write the report
write.description: A written deliverable — usually 30-50 pages. Specific findings, prioritized recommendations, decision criteria. No fluff. We send the draft, you challenge it, we revise once.

report.title: Written report
report.description: 30-50 pages of structured analysis. PDF + Markdown source so your team can reference it forever.

diagram.title: Architecture diagram
diagram.description: One canonical diagram of your current system, plus 1-2 future-state diagrams. Source files included.

roadmap.title: Implementation roadmap
roadmap.description: Phased plan with effort estimates, dependencies, and risk assessment per phase. Editable so your team can adjust.

walkthrough.title: Live walkthrough
walkthrough.description: 90-minute session where we walk you through the report and answer questions. Recorded so people who couldn't attend can watch later.

anti.title: Five things we steer clients AWAY from.
anti.subtitle: We get hired to tell the truth. These are the recommendations we refuse to make, even when the client wants them.

items.a1: GraphQL for systems with 3 or fewer client types.
items.a2: Microservices for engineering teams under 10 people.
items.a3: Custom auth instead of Auth0 / Clerk / Stytch — except in very specific compliance cases.
items.a4: Multi-cloud unless there's a specific regulatory or vendor-risk reason.
items.a5: Custom AI training when an off-the-shelf API will do the job at 10× the price.

cost.question: How much does a consulting engagement cost?
cost.answer: A standard consulting engagement (architecture review, build-vs-buy analysis, AI strategy) is fixed-price between €15K and €40K depending on scope. Larger modernization roadmaps for complex enterprise systems run €40K–€80K. We never bill by the hour for consulting — the price is the price.

timeline.question: How long does an engagement take?
timeline.answer: Standard engagements: 2 weeks from kickoff to written report. Larger modernization roadmaps: 3–4 weeks. We don't drag engagements out — the value is in fast, decisive judgment, not in how long we hang around.

remote.question: Can you do this remotely?
remote.answer: Yes. Most engagements run fully remote — we do code review remotely, interviews via video, the report is a written deliverable. We can come on-site for the kickoff and walkthrough sessions if useful.

implementation.question: Do you implement what you recommend?
implementation.answer: Sometimes. We're a full engineering team, so if you want us to implement after the consulting engagement we can. But there's no obligation — many clients take our report and execute it with their internal team. We're paid for the judgment, not for keeping you dependent.

industries.question: Which industries do you advise on?
industries.answer: Healthcare, fintech, logistics, manufacturing, retail, real estate, education, government, agriculture. We're industry-agnostic on the engineering judgment side, but we always pair the advice with our delivery experience in your sector when relevant.

confidentiality.question: How do you handle confidentiality?
confidentiality.answer: Every engagement starts with a mutual NDA. Code reviews happen on read-only repository access or NDA-protected snapshots. We don't share findings between clients, ever. The report is yours — we keep no copies after delivery if you ask us to.

hero.titleLine1: We assume you are already compromised.
hero.titleLine2: Then we engineer for the day after.
hero.subtitle: Security audits, penetration testing, infrastructure hardening, compliance frameworks. We don't sell paranoia. We engineer the systems and processes that survive a real attack.

threats.title: Six attack patterns. Six defensive postures.
threats.subtitle: Most enterprise breaches fall into one of six patterns. We engineer specific defensive controls against each one.

ransomware.title: Ransomware & data encryption
ransomware.description: Attackers encrypt your data and demand payment for the key. Most ransomware enters through phishing, RDP, or unpatched VPNs.
ransomware.defense: Immutable backups · network segmentation · EDR · phishing simulation

supply.title: Supply chain attacks
supply.description: A trusted dependency (npm package, Docker image, vendor) gets compromised. The attacker rides the trust into your production.
supply.defense: SBOM · image signing (Cosign) · supply-chain attestation · dependency pinning

identity.title: Credential theft & identity attacks
identity.description: Stolen credentials, MFA bypass, session hijacking, OAuth abuse. The most common entry point in modern breaches.
identity.defense: Zero trust · phishing-resistant MFA · short-lived tokens · IAM hardening

web.title: Web & API exploitation
web.description: OWASP Top 10 — injection, broken auth, IDOR, SSRF, deserialization. Most public-facing apps have at least one of these.
web.defense: Code review · ASVS testing · WAF · runtime application self-protection

insider.title: Insider threats & privilege abuse
insider.description: An employee, contractor, or compromised account exfiltrates data or plants backdoors. Hard to detect because the access is legitimate.
insider.defense: Least-privilege · audit logging · UEBA · separation of duties

cloud.title: Cloud misconfiguration
cloud.description: Public S3 buckets, over-permissive IAM, exposed Kubernetes APIs, leaked secrets in code. The #1 cause of cloud-era breaches.
cloud.defense: CSPM · IAM review · secrets scanning · network policies · automated drift detection

compliance.title: Six frameworks. We help you reach all of them.
compliance.subtitle: Compliance is engineering, not paperwork. We build the controls before the auditors show up.

iso27001.description: International standard for managing information security. Process-driven, audited annually. We help build the ISMS, document controls, and prepare for certification.

soc2.description: Required for B2B SaaS sales in the US. Type 1 = controls in place; Type 2 = controls effective over time. We engineer the controls and prepare evidence.

gdpr.description: EU privacy regulation with global reach. Data subject rights, breach notification, lawful basis for processing. We help you map data flows and implement controls.

pcidss.description: Required for any system handling card data. Network segmentation, encryption, access controls, quarterly scans. We engineer the cardholder data environment.

hipaa.description: US healthcare data regulation. Required for systems handling PHI. We help with technical safeguards, audit trails, encryption at rest and in transit.

nis2.description: Newest EU directive — applies to essential and important entities across critical sectors. Risk management, incident reporting, supply chain security.

engagement.title: Four phases. Audit. Pentest. Harden. Monitor.

audit.title: Security audit
audit.description: Scope your environment, threat-model, review code and infrastructure, identify the highest-leverage risks. Output: written audit report with prioritized findings and remediation roadmap.

pentest.title: Penetration test
pentest.description: Authorized adversarial testing of web, mobile, API, cloud, or network targets. Real exploitation, real evidence. Output: detailed report with proof-of-concept and CVSS scores per finding.

harden.title: Hardening & remediation
harden.description: Implement the controls identified in audit and pentest. Code fixes, infrastructure changes, IAM cleanup, network segmentation, secrets rotation, CI/CD hardening.

monitor.title: Continuous monitoring
monitor.description: SIEM, alerting, on-call, monthly reports, quarterly re-tests. Security is not a project — it's an operational capability that needs ongoing engineering.

proof.mainLabel: Confirmed breaches across the Symloop client base, since 2020
proof.context: Across every client we operate or have hardened, the count of confirmed breaches is zero. This is not a marketing number — it is the only number that matters in cybersecurity.

cost.question: How much does a security audit or pentest cost?
cost.answer: A scoped security audit starts at €8K and runs up to €30K depending on environment size. A penetration test is priced by target — a single web app pentest typically lands around €12K–€20K. We start with a fixed-price scoping call (free) before any commitment.

pentest.question: What's the difference between an audit and a pentest?
pentest.answer: An audit is a structured review of your security posture against a framework (OWASP ASVS, ISO 27001, etc.). A pentest is authorized adversarial testing — we actively try to exploit the system. Most clients need both: audit to find systemic gaps, pentest to validate the fixes.

compliance.question: Can you get us SOC 2 or ISO 27001 certified?
compliance.answer: We don't issue certificates — only accredited auditors can do that. But we engineer the controls, document the policies, and prepare you for the audit. Most clients reach SOC 2 Type 1 in 4–6 months and Type 2 in 12–18 months with us as the engineering partner.

incident.question: Do you handle incident response?
incident.answer: Yes. We offer incident response retainers — committed response times for confirmed security incidents. Triage, containment, forensics, post-incident review, and (if needed) coordination with law enforcement and regulators.

remediation.question: Do you also fix the issues you find?
remediation.answer: Yes. We don't believe in audit reports that just say 'fix this'. We do the audit, write the remediation plan, and (if you want) implement the fixes ourselves. Same engineering team — no handoff to a third party.

training.question: Do you train internal teams?
training.answer: Yes. Secure-coding training, phishing simulations, tabletop incident exercises, security champions programs. Best done alongside an audit so the training is grounded in your real findings, not generic content.

cost.question: How much does a custom hardware project cost?
cost.answer: Hardware engineering is more capital-intensive than pure software. A typical custom embedded product (PCB design + firmware + small production batch) starts at €40K and ranges up to €250K depending on certification requirements, production volume, and integration complexity. We start with a fixed-price discovery (1–2 weeks) followed by a fixed-price prototype (4–8 weeks).

manufacturing.question: Where do you actually manufacture?
manufacturing.answer: We design and manufacture in Algeria. PCB fabrication for prototype runs is done locally. Higher-volume production can be split between local assembly (for the Algerian and MENA markets) and certified contract manufacturers in China or Eastern Europe (for European delivery). We handle the manufacturing logistics — you don't have to.

protocols.question: Which connectivity protocols do you support?
protocols.answer: Whatever fits the deployment: WiFi, Bluetooth Low Energy, LoRa/LoRaWAN, Sigfox, 4G/LTE-M, NB-IoT, Zigbee, Z-Wave, MQTT, CoAP. We pick the protocol based on range, power budget, and data volume — not based on what we know best.

fleet.question: How do you manage devices in the field?
fleet.answer: Every Symloop device reports its health back to a central cloud platform. We provide a web dashboard for real-time monitoring, OTA firmware updates, remote diagnostics, and alerting. Failures are caught before they become outages. Most issues are resolved remotely without an on-site visit.

compliance.question: Do you handle certifications (CE, FCC)?
compliance.answer: Yes. We design with certification in mind from day one — EMC compliance, safety, RF compliance. We work with accredited testing labs in Europe and the Gulf for CE/FCC marking. We've shipped CE-marked devices to European clients.

lifetime.question: How long do your devices stay in production?
lifetime.answer: We design for 5–10 year deployments. Firmware updates remain supported for the lifetime of the product. Hardware components are selected for long-term availability — no obsolete parts in our BOMs. Our oldest deployed devices have been running continuously for over 1,200 days.

cost.answer: Engagements are typically structured as fixed-price discovery (1–2 weeks) followed by either a fixed-price migration project (4–12 weeks) or a monthly SRE retainer (€8K–€25K/month depending on scope). sla.question: What SLA can you commit to?
sla.answer: Default production SLA is 99.9% uptime measured rolling 30 days. Higher tiers (99.95%, 99.99%) are available for engagements with multi-region architecture and dedicated on-call rotations. We never sell uptime numbers we can't engineer.

migration.question: Can you migrate us to Kubernetes / cloud / multi-cloud?
migration.answer: Yes. We do incremental migrations (strangler-fig), not big-bang rewrites. Typical migration to Kubernetes takes 6–12 weeks for a mid-sized application with zero production downtime. Multi-cloud takes longer because the tradeoffs need careful design — we'll tell you in discovery if you actually need multi-cloud or if it's complexity you can avoid.

oncall.question: Do you handle on-call?

multicloud.question: AWS, GCP, Azure, or multi-cloud?
multicloud.answer: Whichever fits the workload. We've shipped production systems on all three. We default to single-cloud unless there's a real reason for multi-cloud (regulatory, latency, vendor risk). Most teams who think they need multi-cloud actually need better architecture on one cloud.

security.question: Do you handle security and compliance?
security.answer: Yes. Secrets management, network policies, RBAC, image scanning, supply-chain security, audit logs, compliance frameworks (SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA where applicable). Security is engineered in, not bolted on at audit time.

cost.question: How much does a mobile app cost?
cost.answer: Mobile app pricing depends on platform count, complexity, and integration depth. A focused single-platform pilot starts at €25K. A full cross-platform app (iOS + Android) typically lands between €60K and €200K. We start with a fixed-price discovery (1–2 weeks) followed by a fixed-price pilot before committing to a full build. The discovery delivers a transparent quote — no estimate-by-vibes.

timeline.question: How long does a mobile app take?
timeline.answer: A pilot delivers a working app in 6–10 weeks. A full production app reaches the App Store / Play Store in 16–24 weeks for cross-platform, or 20–28 weeks for native iOS + native Android in parallel. Embedded and hardware-bound apps vary depending on hardware integration scope.

platforms.question: Should we go native or cross-platform?
platforms.answer: It depends. Go native (iOS + Android separately) when platform-specific OS features matter, when your audience is heavily skewed to one platform, or when the app must feel premium. Go cross-platform (Flutter, React Native) when speed, budget, and consistency matter more than platform-specific polish. We help you decide in discovery — and we'll tell you honestly if cross-platform is the wrong call for your case.

offline.question: Do you build offline-first apps?
offline.answer: Yes — it's a default, not a feature. Every Symloop mobile app is designed to work without an internet connection: local-first storage, queued mutations, sync on reconnect, conflict resolution. This is non-negotiable in markets where signal is unreliable.

store.question: Do you handle App Store and Play Store submission?
store.answer: Yes. We handle the full submission process: store listings, metadata, screenshots, app review responses, staged rollouts, hotfix deployment. We've shipped apps to both stores in 9+ regional markets. If you don't have store accounts yet, we help you set them up.

maintenance.question: Do you maintain apps after launch?
maintenance.answer: Yes. We offer ongoing operations contracts: crash triage, performance monitoring, OS-update compatibility (iOS major releases, Android API targets), feature work, security patches. Or we can hand off cleanly to your internal team with documentation and a knowledge transfer.
